testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
recommended_experience: CompTIA Network+ and two years of experience working in a security/ systems administrator job role

prerequisites.description: two years of experience working in a security/ systems administrator job role

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Security controls
sub_objectives: comparing technical, preventive, managerial, deterrent, operational, detective, physical, corrective, compensating, and directive controls.

objectives.title: Fundamental concepts
sub_objectives: summarizing confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA); non-repudiation; authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA); zero trust; and deception/disruption technology.

objectives.title: Change management
sub_objectives: explaining business processes, technical implications, documentation, and version control.

objectives.title: Cryptographic solutions
sub_objectives: using public key infrastructure (PKI), encryption, obfuscation, hashing, digital signatures, and blockchain.

objectives.title: Threat actors and motivations
sub_objectives: comparing nation-states, unskilled attackers, hacktivists, insider threats, organized crime, shadow IT, and motivations like data exfiltration, espionage, and financial gain.

objectives.title: Threat vectors and attack surfaces
sub_objectives: explaining message-based, unsecure networks, social engineering, file-based, voice call, supply chain, and vulnerable software vectors.

objectives.title: Vulnerabilities
sub_objectives: explaining application, hardware, mobile device, virtualization, operating system (OS)-based, cloud-specific, web-based, and supply chain vulnerabilities.

objectives.title: Malicious activity
sub_objectives: analyzing malware attacks, password attacks, application attacks, physical attacks, network attacks, and cryptographic attacks.

objectives.title: Mitigation techniques
sub_objectives: using segmentation, access control, configuration enforcement, hardening, isolation, and patching.

objectives.title: Architecture models
sub_objectives: comparing on-premises, cloud, virtualization, Internet of Things (IoT), industrial control systems (ICS), and infrastructure as code (IaC).

objectives.title: Enterprise infrastructure
sub_objectives: applying security principles to infrastructure considerations, control selection, and secure communication/access.

objectives.title: Data protection
sub_objectives: comparing data types, securing methods, general considerations, and classifications.

objectives.title: Resilience and recovery
sub_objectives: explaining high availability, site considerations, testing, power, platform diversity, backups, and continuity of operations

objectives.title: Computing resources
sub_objectives: applying secure baselines, mobile solutions, hardening, wireless security, application security, sandboxing, and monitoring.

objectives.title: Asset management
sub_objectives: explaining acquisition, disposal, assignment, and monitoring/tracking of hardware, software, and data assets.

objectives.title: Vulnerability management
sub_objectives: identifying, analyzing, remediating, validating, and reporting vulnerabilities.

objectives.title: Alerting and monitoring
sub_objectives: explaining monitoring tools and computing resource activities.

objectives.title: Enterprise security
sub_objectives: modifying firewalls, IDS/IPS, DNS filtering, DLP (data loss prevention), NAC (network access control), and EDR/XDR (endpoint/extended detection and response).

objectives.title: Identity and access management
sub_objectives: implementing provisioning, SSO (single sign-on), MFA (multifactor authentication), and privileged access tools.

objectives.title: Automation and orchestration
sub_objectives: explaining automation use cases, scripting benefits, and considerations.

objectives.title: Incident response
sub_objectives: implementing processes, training, testing, root cause analysis, threat hunting, and digital forensics.

objectives.title: Data sources
sub_objectives: using log data and other sources to support investigations.

objectives.title: Security governance
sub_objectives: summarizing guidelines, policies, standards, procedures, external considerations, monitoring, governance structures, and roles/responsibilities.

objectives.title: Risk management
sub_objectives: explaining risk identification, assessment, analysis, register, tolerance, appetite, strategies, reporting, and business impact analysis (BIA).

objectives.title: Third-party risk
sub_objectives: managing vendor assessment, selection, agreements, monitoring, questionnaires, and rules of engagement.

objectives.title: Security compliance
sub_objectives: summarizing compliance reporting, consequences of non-compliance, monitoring, and privacy.

objectives.title: Audits and assessments
sub_objectives: explaining attestation, internal/external audits, and penetration testing.

objectives.title: Security awareness
sub_objectives: implementing phishing training, anomalous behavior recognition, user guidance, reporting, and monitoring.

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
